title: Medication Review split scores should ignore non-current medications and aggregate consistently
description:
As a SelectQuote HCS QA stakeholder, I want the split Medication Review scores to evaluate only medications the customer currently takes and for the composite to match the sub-scores, so that client feedback and reporting reflect the real review behavior instead of penalizing discontinued medications or showing contradictory composite results.

Feature: Medication Review split scoring respects current-medication status and consistent aggregation

Scenario: Discontinued medication does not cause split-score failure
Given a call where a medication is mentioned and the customer clearly says they no longer take it or that it was only a completed short-course medication
When Medication Review split scores are computed
Then that medication is excluded from dosage and prescriber failure counting
And the agent is not penalized for not confirming dosage or prescriber for that non-current medication

Scenario: Composite matches all passing sub-scores
Given a call where Medication Review: Prescriber, Dosage, Pharmacy, and Individual Confirmation all return Yes
When Medication Review (Composite) is computed
Then the composite returns Yes
And the explanation indicates that all elements passed

Scenario: Composite lists failed split elements
Given a call where one or more Medication Review split scores return No
When Medication Review (Composite) is computed
Then the composite returns No
And the explanation names the failed split elements only.
